person.name: Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto

nameForms.fullText: Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto

birthPlace.address: Kirkcudbright Castle, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Robert de le Glen was likely born c. 1300, at the family estates at Lochwinnoc in Renfrewshire. There is no definitive source for his birth although he appears to have been married prior to Bruce's death. If the legend holds true regarding accompanying Douglas in his journey to Palestine the marriage likely occurred c. 1325.

There is very little on record of Robert. He does not appear on the People of medieval Scotland dataset, although is at the end of that period, and does not appear in the Record of the Parliament of Scotland, suggesting he was not a Lord. From what we do know:

He married Margaret, a illegitimate daughter of Robert Bruce by an unknown mother. This marriage likely occurring just prior to when Bruce became incapable or just after he died. Likely c. 1325. She was still alive 29 February 1364. Woods edition of the Scots Peerage suggests she was Margaret, a legitimate daughter and married secondly, the Earl of Sutherland, but this is an error.

Robert de Glen and "Margaret Bruce the King's sister," his spouse, had a grant from David II., undated, but likely c. 1330 of Nether Pitedye at Kinghorn in Fife. These lands adjoin their estates at Balmuto.

Robertson, in his work on the Lost Charters, notes three other charters from David to this Robert de Glen, of the lands of Glasgow Forest, thanedom of Kintore, now Aberdeenshire.

Family tradition, traceable for four centuries, insists that Robert de Glen was one of those who accompanied Douglas during his journey with the heart of Bruce to the Holy Land, and the Glens of the Linlithgow line used two crests, one a martlet; the other an arm, the hand grasping a heart, in commemoration of that event. The sword of Bruce was also supposedly retained within the family. If this is so Glen is not mentioned amongst the members of Douglas' party although that in itself is not definitive.

«b»Family«/b»
«b»Robert de Glen and Margaret Bruce had issue, three sons at least, although the order is not certain:«/b»
1.) William Glen. William appears to have inherited the paternal estates, including lands in the lordship of Glen, and in Kilmun, save Balmuto and adjacent property, suggesting the eldest son. William died before 14 June 1373, at which time Paul de Glen, his son and heir, was of age.

2.)Sir John Glen. Seemingly the second son as he gains the maternal lands. He gains, seemingly by enfeoffment, Balmuto and its surrounding lands; those granted as a result of the marriage to Margaret Bruce. He will be the father of a Sir John de Glen, of Balmuto. This Sir John was party to a deed dated after 1373, between Sir John de Glen de Balmuto, and Margaret his wife, and Sir John de Wemyess and Isabel his wife, touching lands of Sir John de Glen in exchange for lands of Sir John de Wemyess, the latter lying north of the Firth of Forth.

3.) Sir Robert Glen. Robert de Glen entered the Church. He became Rector of Liberton in Lanarkshire, the living being in gift of John, Lord of Maxwell, as appears by a charter of the latter, and Christian his wife, 12 October, 1357, to the monastery of Kilwynnyn, of patronage or advowson of the said church, with one acre of land, reserving the rights of Sir Robert de Glen, the Rector.

«b»Arms«/b»
It is worth noting that some historians suggest that arms of this family are derived from those of Abernethy; the lion rampant. However the lion rampant are also evidenced in the arms of Glen and at a contemporary date. While Abernethy are or, a lion rampant gules; those of Glen were argent, a lion rampant sable. There is notice of a shield, dated c. 1415, of a Sir John Glen where his shield lies within octagonal ornament. This might suggest the bordure gobony, recognition of the mark of illegitimacy born on Scottish Arms, and arising from this marriage.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto於大約1308年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾時，他的父親Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen34歲，母親Elizabeth Abernethy31歲。他於1334年11月10日與Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,在英國蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1349年在美國南達科他州傑克遜卡素享年43歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭費伏。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto于大约1308年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾时，他的父亲Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen34岁，母亲Elizabeth Abernethy31岁。他于1334年11月10日与Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,在英国苏格兰吉德布萊次爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1349年在美国南达科他州傑克遜卡素享年43岁，埋葬在苏格兰費伏。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Glenn,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Derry and Galway): from Mag Fhloinn, see McGlynn .

variants.name: McGlynn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Bridie, Colm, Declan, Donovan, Sean Patrick, Thomas Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Fhloinn, a patronymic from the personal name Flann ‘red, crimson’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto was born about 1308, in Renfrewshire, Scotland, his father, Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen, was 34 and his mother, Elizabeth Abernethy, was 31. He married Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, on 10 November 1334, in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1349, in Castle, Jackson, South Dakota, United States, at the age of 43, and was buried in Fife, Scotland.

fr.Balmuto Castle, Fife, Scotland: Castle, Jackson, Dakota du Sud, États-Unis
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto est né environ 1308, à Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Abernethy, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, le 10 novembre 1334, à Kirkcudbrightshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1349, à Castle, Jackson, Dakota du Sud, États-Unis, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Fife, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto um 1308 in Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen 34 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Abernethy 31. Er hat am 10. November 1334 in Kirkcudbrightshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1349 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Castle, Jackson, South Dakota, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Fife, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmutoが約1308年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glenは34歳，母Elizabeth Abernethyは31歳であった。1334年11月10日にイギリススコットランドキルククウドブリトシュアイアでLady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1349年にアメリカ合衆国サウスダコタ州ジャクソンカストルで43歳で死去し，スコットランドフィフにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto(이)가 대략 1308년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Abernethy(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1334년 11월 10일 영국 스코틀랜드 커크쿠드브라이츠히르에서 Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1349년 미국 사우스다코타 주 잭슨 카스틀에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 피프에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto родился около 1308 г. в местности ренфрюшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Abernethy, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, 10 ноября 1334 г. в местности кирккудбраицхир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1349 г. в местности кастл, джекксон, Южная Дакота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности фиф, Шотландия.
